summary.scaffold_decision: Use this scaffold to draft a conservative maturity/gap paper before opening public browser or AWS positive-result gates.

sections.purpose: State the paper as a conservative maturity/gap evaluation instead of a guarantee claim.
sections.use_en: We assess how QUIC/HTTP/3 migration primitives, deployment routing, browser behavior, and workload design shape application-level continuity.
sections.use_ko: QUIC/HTTP/3 migration primitive, 배포 라우팅, 브라우저 동작, workload 설계가 애플리케이션 수준 작업 연속성에 만드는 경계를 평가한다고 쓴다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not claim that HTTP/3 CM guarantees seamless continuity.
sections.next_gap: If a stronger abstract is desired, open public Chrome or AWS positive-result gates first.

sections.purpose: Separate path change, local rebinding, deployment routing, browser policy, and app recovery terminology.
sections.use_en: Motivate the problem as a layer-boundary question: transport migration support does not automatically imply browser-visible task continuity.
sections.use_ko: 문제 제기는 계층 경계 문제로 잡는다. transport migration 지원이 곧 browser-visible task continuity를 뜻하지 않는다고 설명한다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not use broad unstable mobile network wording without defining the tested path-change class.
sections.next_gap: Finalize terminology with the professor before writing the abstract and introduction.

sections.purpose: Explain implementation maturity as evidence-level classification rather than a binary support label.
sections.use_en: Classify implementation evidence into runtime controls, app demos, source/test audits, deployment gates, and negative controls.
sections.use_ko: 구현체 근거를 runtime control, app demo, source/test audit, deployment gate, negative control로 나누어 설명한다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not state that all surveyed implementations are equally mature.
sections.next_gap: Run large production-stack focused tests only if the appendix needs more implementation depth.

sections.purpose: Define conservative strong-CM acceptance criteria for public Chrome rows.
sections.use_en: Require application completion, client active path change, target tuple change, qlog path validation, and one Chrome target QUIC session in the same active row.
sections.use_ko: 동일 active row에서 application completion, client active path change, target tuple change, qlog path validation, Chrome target QUIC session 1개를 모두 요구한다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not count task completion alone as Connection Migration success.
sections.next_gap: Open public H3 origin and non-iPhone desktop path gates before claiming public Chrome CM success.

sections.purpose: Report that implementation primitives exist across stacks while behavior and deployability differ.
sections.use_en: Implementation evidence shows CM-related primitives are present across multiple stacks, but API exposure, observability, and deployment readiness differ.
sections.use_ko: 여러 구현체에 CM 관련 primitive는 존재하지만 API 노출, 관찰성, 배포 readiness는 구현체마다 다르다고 보고한다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not generalize a quic-go positive control to all stacks or browser behavior.
sections.next_gap: Use implementation evidence as foundation, not as final web-continuity proof.

sections.purpose: Report CID-aware deployment and proxy negative-control boundaries.
sections.use_en: Deployment results should distinguish CID-aware routing, proxy termination boundaries, local prerequisites, and blocked live AWS execution.
sections.use_ko: 배포 결과는 CID-aware routing, proxy termination boundary, local prerequisite, blocked live AWS execution을 분리해 쓴다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not claim live AWS NLB+s2n forwarding or active migration success yet.
sections.next_gap: Refresh AWS credentials to run live forwarding before any AWS positive claim.

sections.purpose: Use local Chrome controls to prioritize workloads while avoiding public handover overclaim.
sections.use_en: Local Chrome forced-H3 controls show that range/download and upload provide cleaner single-session local evidence than streaming-like workloads.
sections.use_ko: Local Chrome forced-H3 control에서는 range/download와 upload가 streaming형 workload보다 단일 session local evidence가 더 선명하다고 쓴다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not call local rebinding a public Wi-Fi/LTE or desktop-interface handover result.
sections.next_gap: Run controlled-public range/upload trials first once public-origin and path-change gates open.

sections.purpose: Frame streaming as QoE/session-attribution evidence rather than zero-impact continuity.
sections.use_en: Streaming outcomes must include rebuffering, startup delay, retry behavior, and Chrome target session count.
sections.use_ko: Streaming 결과는 rebuffering, startup delay, retry behavior, Chrome target session count를 함께 보고한다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not treat playback completion alone as continuity or single-session CM.
sections.next_gap: Public streaming trials need QoE metrics and session attribution together.

sections.purpose: Make blocked public Chrome, AWS, Safari, and terminology gaps explicit.
sections.use_en: Limitations should state that no tracked active public Chrome row satisfies strong CM success, live AWS+s2n is credential-blocked, and Safari remains feasibility-only.
sections.use_ko: 한계에서는 tracked active public Chrome strong CM success가 없고, live AWS+s2n은 credential-blocked이며, Safari는 feasibility-only라고 명시한다.
sections.do_not_claim: Do not hide the absence of public/browser positive rows.
sections.next_gap: Professor decision should choose maturity/gap scope or external positive-result gate work.
